message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>BACKGROUND</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Constant mutation in the SARS-COV2 virus genetic material is resulting in the appearance of new variants frequently hence the overall virulence, treatment resistance, replication modalities, transmissions rates and COVID-19 signs & symptoms are all changing regularly.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>METHODOLOGY</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    From 1 January 2021 to 30 August 2022, the clinical lab at Fatima Jinnah General & Chest Hospital Quetta, Balochistan, determined a total of 3375 individuals to be COVID-19 positive because RT-PCR detected ORF, N, and E genes or their various Bi & Tri combinations in their samples. A questionnaire-based interview was conducted with each participant during sample collection. Body temperature more than 37
                    <jats:sup>0</jats:sup>
                    c was recorded as Fever/Chill. Age, Comorbidities, A-symptomatic individuals & Vaccination status were all neglected during this study. Frequency tables were generated using MS-excel 2016, while Odds ratios were calculated using Chi-square test of association whereby 2×2 contingency tables between Mono, Bi & Tri combinations for ORF, N & E genes were cross associated with various generalized nonspecific COVID -19 signs and symptoms using Epi-info software. Absence of Genetic sequencing was the major limitation.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>RESULTS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The study showed that individually the presence of
                    <jats:bold>ORF gene</jats:bold>
                    was found to be strongly associated
                    <jats:bold>“ Shortness of Breath/Difficulty in Breathing”, Diarrhea, Head ache & Vomitting</jats:bold>
                    . While the presence of
                    <jats:bold>N- gene</jats:bold>
                    was found to be strongly associated with
                    <jats:bold>Loss of smell & taste, Head ache</jats:bold>
                    ,
                    <jats:bold>Presistant Chest Pain & Bluish lips/Face</jats:bold>
                    . Where as the presence of
                    <jats:bold>E-gene</jats:bold>
                    was found to be strongly associated with
                    <jats:bold>Cough, Shortness of breath/ Difficulty in breathing, Sore throat, Diarrhea, Head ache & Laziness</jats:bold>
                    . In addition, the study also found that different Bi & Tri combinations of ORF, N & E genes in a COVID-19 positive patient expressed generalized non-specific COVID-19 signs & symptoms differently.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>DISCUSSION & CONCLUSION</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The presence of various SARS-COV2 genetic markers significantly alters the clinical presentation of COVID-19.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: STRENGTH OF ASSOCIATION BETWEEN GENERALIZED/NONSPECIFIC COVID-19 SIGNS & SYMPTOMS WITH SARS-COV 2 SPECIFIC ORF, N, E GENES IDENTIFIED THROUGH REAL TIME PCR
